name: OVO Indonesia E-Wallet Payment API
what_it_does: OVO Indonesia digital wallet REST API for merchants to accept mobile wallet payments, QRIS QR codes, and OVO Cash payments from Indonesia's popular e-wallet with 115M+ users backed by Grab and Tokopedia ecosystem for e-commerce, in-app, and merchant payment integration across Indonesia. Enables AI agents to manage OVO payment request creation for Indonesia wallet checkout automation, handle QRIS QR generation for Indonesia in-store merchant payment automation, access OVO Cash payment for Indonesia digital wallet automation, retrieve payment status and confirmation for Indonesia transaction automation, manage refund processing for Indonesia order management automation, handle notification webhook for Indonesia payment event automation, access OVO Points loyalty integration for Indonesia rewards automation, retrieve merchant settlement for Indonesia reconciliation automation, manage OVO PayLater for Indonesia BNPL checkout automation, and integrate OVO with Indonesian e-commerce, Tokopedia, Grab, and fintech platforms for end-to-end Indonesia OVO ecosystem payment automation.
use_cases: Accepting OVO wallet payments from Indonesia e-commerce checkout agents
Generating QRIS codes for Indonesia in-store OVO merchants from POS agents
Processing OVO PayLater BNPL from Indonesia consumer finance agents
Confirming OVO payment notifications from Indonesia transaction agents
not_for: Bank transfer payments (use DOKU or Midtrans for Indonesia VA bank transfer)
Credit card processing (use Midtrans or Xendit for Indonesia card acceptance)
best_when: An Indonesian merchant or Tokopedia/Grab ecosystem partner wanting AI agents to automate OVO wallet payment acceptance, QRIS QR generation, and payment confirmation through Indonesia's second-largest e-wallet platform.
avoid_when: MERCHANT PARTNER API REQUIRES FORMAL AGREEMENT: OVO merchant API requires partnership with PT Visionet International (OVO); automated self-service API integration without partnership creates unauthorized access. QRIS FOLLOWS BANK INDONESIA STANDARD: OVO QRIS implementation follows Bank Indonesia QRIS standard; automated QR must be QRIS-compliant; automated proprietary QR creates non-interoperable QR for non-OVO apps. PAYLATER REQUIRES CONSUMER CREDIT ASSESSMENT: OVO PayLater approval depends on consumer creditworthiness; automated PayLater checkout must handle decline and offer alternative; automated assumption of universal PayLater approval creates dead-end checkout for declined consumers. TOKOPEDIA AND GRAB INTEGRATION DIFFERS: OVO within Tokopedia and Grab apps uses embedded SDK not REST API; automated integration within these super apps requires platform-specific integration; automated REST API call within super app context creates incompatible payment flow.

auth.notes: OVO uses Client ID + Client Secret with HMAC signing for merchant API. REST API with JSON. Jakarta, Indonesia HQ. PT Visionet International. Backed by Grab, Tokopedia, Grab. 115M+ registered users. Products: E-wallet, QRIS, OVO PayLater, OVO Points. OJK-licensed. PCI DSS. Indonesia's second-largest e-wallet. Competes with DANA and GoPay for Indonesia digital wallet.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — merchant partnership required
pricing.paid_tiers: Transaction fee 0.7-2.0% per transaction; QRIS: 0.7% MDR per Bank Indonesia regulation
pricing.notes: Jakarta ID. Grab/Tokopedia ecosystem. Per-transaction fees in IDR. QRIS MDR regulated by Bank Indonesia.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per merchant; payment and query APIs throttled; webhooks for payment events

known_agent_gotchas: HMAC SIGNATURE REQUIRED FOR ALL REQUESTS: OVO merchant API requires HMAC signature on all requests using Client Secret; automated request must compute HMAC signature; automated unsigned request creates authentication failure

UNIQUE REFERENCE NUMBER PER TRANSACTION: OVO requires unique reference number per payment; automated retry must generate new reference number; automated reference reuse creates duplicate transaction rejected

NOTIFICATION ENDPOINT MUST RETURN 200: OVO payment notification requires HTTP 200 acknowledgment within timeout; automated notification handler must respond quickly; automated slow or error response creates OVO retry loop
INDONESIAN RUPIAH INTEGER AMOUNTS: OVO amounts are in full Indonesian Rupiah without decimal; automated amount must be integer; automated decimal IDR amount creates invalid amount error

QRIS MDR APPLIES TO ALL MERCHANT CATEGORIES: OVO QRIS transactions incur Bank Indonesia-mandated MDR (merchant discount rate) of 0.7% for MSMEs; automated merchant MDR calculation must account for QRIS-specific rate; automated generic flat-rate assumption creates incorrect merchant fee calculation
agent_readiness.security_notes: Indonesia e-wallet. OJK-licensed, PCI-DSS. HMAC signing. ID. Mobile payment data. Grab/Tokopedia ecosystem.

security_score_components.security_notes: Indonesia e-wallet. OJK-licensed. HMAC signing. ID. Mobile payment data.
